h2h_summary: Senegal and Iraq have no verified competitive head-to-head history; this 2026 World Cup group stage match is believed to be their first major tournament meeting.
wc_history_home: Senegal have appeared at three World Cups (2002, 2018, 2022), with their finest hour a stunning 2002 debut that knocked out defending champions France and reached the quarter-finals — their best-ever finish.
wc_history_away: Iraq appeared at the World Cup just once before, at Mexico 1986, where they lost all three group games and scored a single goal, making their 2026 participation a historic second appearance after a 40-year absence.
group_context: Group I features France, Norway, Senegal, and Iraq; France and Norway are heavy favourites for the top two spots, meaning this Senegal vs Iraq clash is effectively a must-win for Senegal to stay in contention for second place and knockout qualification.
strengths_home: Star-studded attack with pace and physicality across the front three
Solid defensive record — conceded only three goals in African qualifying
AFCON 2021 and 2026 champions with tournament winning experience
strengths_away: Disciplined defensive organisation under Graham Arnold's structured system
Dangerous from set pieces with physical aerial presence
Strong team spirit and counter-attacking threat through Aymen Hussein and Al-Hamadi
team_style_home: Senegal play an energetic 4-3-3 built on high pressing, rapid wing transitions, and Sadio Mane's ability to drop deep and link play, combining physical intensity with technical flair from their European-based attackers.
team_style_away: Iraq under Graham Arnold employ a compact 4-5-1 / 4-4-2 defensive block, prioritizing disciplined low-medium lines, set-piece threat, and fast direct transitions through Aymen Hussein and Ali Al-Hamadi on the counter.
recent_news: Senegal's goalkeeper Édouard Mendy is ruled out of the World Cup match against Iraq due to a knee injury sustained during their recent game against Norway. The match is crucial for both teams as only a victory will keep their hopes alive for reaching the knockout rounds.
key_players_home: Nicolas Jackson (striker, Bayern Munich loan from Chelsea)
key_players_away: Aymen Hussein (striker, national all-time top scorer)
context: Group I features France, Norway, Senegal, and Iraq; France and Norway are heavy favourites for the top two spots, meaning this Senegal vs Iraq clash is effectively a must-win for Senegal to stay in contention for second place and knockout qualification.
